'''Josh Perry''' (born 4 February 1981) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer. An Australian international and [[New South Wales state rugby league team|New South Wales]] State of Origin representative {{rlp|pr}}, he played in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] for the [[Newcastle Knights]], with whom he won the [[2001 NRL Premiership]] and the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]], with whom he won the [[2008 NRL Premiership]], and played in the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].

==Background==
Perry was born in [[Newcastle, New South Wales]], [[Australia]].

Perry attended Caves Beach Public School and his very first Rugby League Club was Swansea Junior Rugby League Club, NSW. He was a Valentine Junior.

His brother James plays for the [[Philadelphia Fight]] in the [[American National Rugby League]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.fightrugby.com/n.php?n=282 |title=PBR Fight 2010 Season-Player preview :: Philadelphia Fight Rugby |access-date=1 May 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110711001609/http://www.fightrugby.com/n.php?n=282 |archive-date=11 July 2011  }}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
====Newcastle Knights====
Perry made his first-grade début in round 17 of the [[National Rugby League season 2000|2000 season]] playing for Newcastle against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] at [[EnergyAustralia Stadium]] on 27 May.<ref name="NRL Stats">{{cite web|publisher=NRL Stats|title=Player Page" Josh Perry|url=http://203.166.101.37/NRL08/playercareer.asp?lplayer=124|access-date=11 October 2008}} {{Dead link|date=October 2010|bot=H3llBot}}</ref>

Perry played at prop forward in the [[2001 NRL Grand Final]]-winning Newcastle team that defeated the [[Parramatta Eels]], 30–24 at [[Stadium Australia]] on 30 September in an upset victory.<ref name="Newcastle Herald">{{cite news|author=Robert Dillon |work=Newcastle Herald|title=Brian Smith happy to see Josh Perry reach potential |url=http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/brian-smith-happy-for-josh-perry/2008/10/02/1222651231579.html?page=fullpage#contentSwap1 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090917030844/http://www.leaguehq.com.au/news/news/brian-smith-happy-for-josh-perry/2008/10/02/1222651231579.html?page=fullpage |url-status=dead |archive-date=17 September 2009 |access-date=11 October 2008 |date=2 October 2008 }}</ref> Having won the [[2001 NRL Premiership]], the Newcastle club traveled to England to play the [[2002 World Club Challenge]] against [[Super League]] champions, the [[Bradford Bulls]]. Perry played at prop forward in Newcastle's loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/08/11/knights-draw-2001-grand-final-inspiration/|title=Knights draw 2001 grand final inspiration|website=NRL.com|date=10 August 2017 }}</ref>

In the [[2005 NRL season]], Perry was limited to only seven appearances for Newcastle as the club endured a horror year on the field and finished last.  In the [[2006 NRL season]], the club turned their fortunes around by qualifying for the finals.  Perry played in the club's 50-6 elimination final loss against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News|access-date=3 January 2020|archive-date=11 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191211141048/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

[[File:Josh Perry.JPG|thumb|right|150px|Perry playing for Manly]]

====Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles====
In 2007, Perry came off contract at the [[Newcastle Knights]] and he signed for the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] for the [[2008 NRL season]] on a three-year contract.<ref name="Newcastle Herald"/>

In his first year with the Sea Eagles, Perry played in the [[2008 NRL Grand Final]] victory over the [[Melbourne Storm]].<ref name="Newcastle Herald"/>

====St Helens====
Perry joined English club [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] for the 2011 season on a three-year contract.<ref name="heraldsun2">{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/josh-perry-is-nrls-latest-loss/story-e6frfgbo-1225865707825|title=Josh Perry is NRL's latest loss|website=Herald Sun|access-date=19 March 2019}}</ref> Perry saw out his contract and retired from the sport after being released at the end of the 2013 season.

===Representative career===
Perry was also selected for [[City vs Country Origin|Country Origin]] in 2001, 2003 and 2004, scoring one try in the 2001 match.

Perry was selected to represent New South Wales as a reserve for game I of the [[2003 State of Origin series]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=Rugby League Project|title=Game I, State of origin 2003|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.com/matches/State_Of_Origin_2003/Game_1/Queensland-vs-New_South_Wales.html|access-date=23 December 2007}}</ref>

In August 2008, Perry was named in the [[Australia national rugby league team#Rugby League World Cup Squad|preliminary 46-man Kangaroos squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite news|author=Liam FitzGibbon |publisher=Fox Sports News (Australia) |title=Surprises in Kangaroos squad |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,24111780-23214,00.html |access-date=2 August 2008 |date=1 August 2008 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080808144730/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0%2C8659%2C24111780-23214%2C00.html |archive-date= 8 August 2008 }}</ref><ref name="Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.iht.com/articles/ap/2008/08/01/sports/AS-RGL-Australia-Squad.php|title=Veteran Lockyer named in Australian squad|publisher=International Herald Tribune|date=1 August 2008|access-date=2 August 2008}}</ref>

Two days following the 2008 NRL grand final, Perry was named in the 24-man [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Australia|Australia]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Newcastle Herald"/><ref name="Manly six win Australia call-up">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/7656211.stm|title=Manly six win Australia call-up|publisher=BBC|date=7 October 2008|access-date=7 October 2008}}</ref> Perry made his test début for [[Australian national rugby league team|Australia]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] from the bench in Australia's opening RLWC 08 game on 26 October.

He was selected for Country in the [[City vs Country Origin|City vs Country]] match on 8 May 2009 and selected in the New South Wales team for game 3 in the 2009 series.<ref>{{cite news|work=[[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]]|title=Blues halfback duel in two as Mitchell Pearce misses selection|first=Todd|last=Balym|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|archive-url=https://archive.today/20121205020103/http://www.foxsports.com.au/story/0,8659,25422326-5016947,00.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=5 December 2012|date=3 May 2009|access-date=3 May 2009}}</ref>

Perry was rewarded for his and Manly's good start to [[2010 NRL season]] with selection on the bench for [[Australian Kangaroos|Australia]] in the [[2010 ANZAC Test]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] in [[Melbourne]] on 7 May.
